questions.question: Which situation best demonstrates balanced forces?

options.A: A book resting on a table
options.B: A ball falling from a tree
options.C: A car accelerating on a road

questions.explanation: The upward support force from the table balances the downward gravitational force.

questions.question: When two forces act on an object in opposite directions and one is greater, what happens?

options.B: The object moves in the direction of the stronger force

questions.explanation: Unbalanced forces cause motion in the direction of the larger force.

questions.question: Which of the following forces acts between the Earth and all objects with mass?
questions.explanation: Gravity acts between all objects that have mass.

questions.question: Why does a rolling ball eventually stop on the ground?
questions.explanation: Friction between the ball and the surface slows it down until it stops.

questions.question: Which factor would most likely increase friction between two surfaces?
questions.explanation: Rougher surfaces produce greater friction.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a non-contact force?
questions.explanation: Friction requires contact between surfaces.

questions.question: Which example best shows electrostatic force?

options.B: A balloon sticking to a wall after rubbing
options.C: A stone falling to the ground

questions.explanation: Static electricity creates electrostatic attraction between the balloon and wall.

questions.question: Which situation demonstrates that force can change the shape of an object?
questions.explanation: Stretching a rubber band changes its shape.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why lubricants are used in machines?

options.B: To reduce friction between moving parts

questions.explanation: Lubricants reduce friction between surfaces.

questions.question: Which example demonstrates unbalanced forces?

options.A: A stationary book on a table
options.B: A tug-of-war where both teams pull equally
options.D: A picture hanging on a wall

questions.explanation: Kicking the football applies an unbalanced force causing motion.

questions.question: Which force is responsible for keeping satellites in orbit around Earth?
questions.explanation: Earth's gravity keeps satellites in orbit.

questions.question: Which situation shows both gravitational force and friction acting together?

options.A: A stone resting on the ground
options.B: A sliding book slowing down on a table

questions.explanation: Gravity pulls the book downward while friction slows its motion.

questions.question: Which type of force acts between two charged objects without contact?
questions.explanation: Electrostatic force acts between electrically charged objects.

questions.question: Which example shows that force can change the speed of an object?
questions.explanation: Braking applies force that reduces speed.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why heavy objects are harder to move?

options.B: They require greater force to overcome inertia

questions.explanation: Objects with greater mass require more force to change motion.

questions.question: Which factor most affects the strength of gravitational force between two objects?
questions.explanation: Greater mass increases gravitational attraction.

questions.question: Why do vehicles have tread patterns on their tires?

options.B: To increase friction with the road

questions.explanation: Treads increase friction and prevent slipping.

questions.question: Which of the following situations shows magnetic repulsion?

options.A: Two magnets pushing away from each other

questions.explanation: Like poles of magnets repel each other.

questions.question: Which example best shows that force can start motion?

options.C: A book resting on a table
options.D: A stone lying on the ground

questions.explanation: Applying force starts motion in a stationary object.

questions.question: Which force acts when we rub a comb on dry hair and attract small paper pieces?
questions.explanation: Rubbing creates static charges that attract paper pieces.

questions.question: Why do objects slide more easily on ice than on rough ground?
questions.explanation: Ice provides a smoother surface with less friction.

questions.question: Which pair of forces act together when a person pushes a heavy box across the floor?
questions.explanation: Muscular force moves the box while friction opposes the motion.

questions.question: Which example demonstrates that friction produces heat?
questions.explanation: Rubbing hands together produces heat due to friction.

questions.question: Which of the following forces acts over the greatest distance?
questions.explanation: Gravitational force can act over very large distances in space.

questions.question: Which example best demonstrates multiple forces acting on an object at the same time?

options.A: A parachutist falling through the air
options.D: A stone lying on the ground

questions.explanation: Both gravity and air resistance act on a falling parachutist.
